_comment: MULTI-ENDPOINT SPEC — NOT a single API payload. Consumed by `install/apply-standards.sh --apply` (PR-C2), which MUST iterate the top-level sections (`general`, `selected_actions`, `workflow`, `access`, `_fork_pr_settings`) and dispatch each to its own `_endpoint` URL. A naive `gh api PUT ... --input @actions-permissions.json` will 422 — the top-level section keys are NOT GitHub API fields.
_apply_note: Before POST/PUT of any inner section, PR-C2 MUST strip ALL `_`-prefix keys (this file has many, and more get added — do NOT maintain a hand-written key list). Use the generic recursive form `install/apply-standards.sh` actually uses: `jq '.<section> | walk(if type == "object" then with_entries(select(.key | startswith("_") | not)) else . end)'`. GitHub REST endpoints strictly validate unknown fields, so a missed key is a 422.

selected_actions._purpose: which third-party actions the repo may run
selected_actions._verified_note: CI-0011 (founder, 2026-07-24) / FT-27/FT-46: FALSE (least-privilege). `verified_allowed: true` admitted EVERY GitHub-verified creator's action (aquasecurity, docker, hashicorp, …) — wider than REPO_STANDARDS §4.3, which restricts to github-owned + the patterns and forced gacts/gitleaks → binary. With this false, only `github_owned_allowed` + `patterns_allowed` admit an action; a verified-creator action outside the patterns is blocked at run-init. NB the deployed boundary (`vladm3105/*`) stays deliberately WIDER than canon's authoring rule (`vladm3105/aidoc-flow-ci/*`) — see REPO_STANDARDS §4.3.
selected_actions._patterns_note: CI-0011: the founder's own account `vladm3105/*` replaces the verified marketplace as the SOLE non-GitHub-owned allowance (broadened from `vladm3105/aidoc-flow-ci/*`, so every canon reusable stays admitted). `actions/*` + `github/*` are GitHub-owned and listed explicitly alongside `github_owned_allowed: true`. Adding any other owner here re-widens the supply-chain boundary — do it deliberately, and record it as a decision.

workflow._can_approve_note: FT-27: default FALSE (least-privilege). GitHub bundles create+approve into this one toggle; it is needed ONLY by the opt-in doc-maintainer/docs-sync bot-PR flows. Harmless under required_approving_review_count: 0, but a standing bypass if that count is ever raised. Flip to true in the bot-PR adoption runbook, not by default.

access._conditional: APPLY ONLY IF repo visibility != public. GitHub 422s this endpoint on public repos ('Access policy only applies to internal and private repositories'). PR-C2 MUST check visibility (via `gh repo view --json isPrivate` or the passed `--visibility` flag) and SKIP this section on public repos. Verified empirically 2026-07-07 on vladm3105/aidoc-flow-ci.

_fork_pr_settings._note: The two fork-PR toggles below (write tokens + secrets) are configured via the repo-level Settings > Actions > General > Fork pull request workflows block. GitHub does NOT expose these via public REST API as of 2026-07 — apply-standards.sh will emit a WARNING pointing to Settings UI for these two. Tracked as v2 (whenever an API endpoint appears).
